items.headline: Arsenal Complete Signing of Viktor Gyökeres for Club-Record Fee
items.summary: Arsenal have finalized a deal to sign Sporting CP striker Viktor Gyökeres for a reported £85 million, making him the most expensive signing in the club's history.

items.headline: Saka Returns to Full Training Ahead of Champions League Quarterfinal
items.summary: Bukayo Saka has been spotted in full team training, boosting Arsenal's hopes ahead of their Champions League clash with Barcelona next week.

items.headline: Arteta: 'We Must Stay Focused on the Premier League Title Race'
items.summary: Mikel Arteta emphasized the importance of maintaining concentration in the league despite their European ambitions, with Arsenal sitting two points clear at the top.

items.headline: Anthropic Launches Claude 4.5 Family with Enhanced Reasoning
items.summary: Anthropic released Claude 4.5 Opus and Sonnet with significantly improved coding, math, and multi-step reasoning capabilities. Early benchmarks show major gains on complex tasks.

items.headline: OpenAI Rolls Out GPT-5 to Enterprise Customers
items.summary: OpenAI has begun a phased rollout of GPT-5 to enterprise API customers, with consumer access expected within two weeks.

items.headline: EU AI Act Enforcement Begins for High-Risk Systems
items.summary: The European Union has started enforcing AI Act requirements for high-risk AI systems, with companies facing potential fines of up to 7% of global revenue for non-compliance.

items.headline: Fed Signals Potential Rate Cut in June as Inflation Cools
items.summary: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell indicated the central bank may begin cutting interest rates as early as June, citing sustained progress on inflation moving toward the 2% target.

items.headline: Stripe Files Confidentially for IPO
items.summary: Stripe has confidentially filed for an initial public offering with the SEC, setting the stage for what could be the largest tech IPO of 2026.

items.headline: Bipartisan Infrastructure Bill Passes Senate with Broadband Funding
items.summary: The Senate passed a $120 billion infrastructure package focused on rural broadband expansion and electric vehicle charging networks, with support from both parties.

items.headline: Supreme Court to Hear Major Case on AI-Generated Content Copyright
items.summary: The Supreme Court agreed to take up a landmark case that will determine whether AI-generated content can be copyrighted, with implications for the entire creative industry.

items.headline: New mRNA Vaccine Shows 94% Efficacy Against All Influenza Strains
items.summary: Moderna announced Phase 3 trial results for a universal flu vaccine using mRNA technology, showing unprecedented cross-strain protection that could eliminate the need for annual reformulation.

items.headline: SpaceX Starship Completes First Orbital Refueling Test
items.summary: SpaceX successfully demonstrated orbital propellant transfer between two Starship vehicles, a critical milestone for NASA's Artemis lunar landing program.

items.headline: Christopher Nolan's 'Odyssey' Dominates Box Office in Opening Weekend
items.summary: Christopher Nolan's adaptation of Homer's Odyssey earned $142 million domestically in its opening weekend, making it the largest opening for a non-franchise film in history.

items.headline: Kendrick Lamar Announces Surprise Album Drop for Friday
items.summary: Kendrick Lamar revealed on social media that his new album will drop this Friday, accompanied by a world tour starting in June.

items.headline: MLB Opening Day: Yankees Debut $700M Roster Against Red Sox
items.summary: The Yankees opened the 2026 season with baseball's most expensive roster ever assembled, facing their historic rivals at Fenway Park.

items.headline: 2026 World Cup Ticket Sales Break Records
items.summary: FIFA announced that ticket sales for the 2026 World Cup in the US, Canada, and Mexico have already surpassed 5 million, with the tournament still two months away.

youtube.title: The AI Tools That Actually Changed My Workflow in 2026
youtube.creator: Matt Wolfe
youtube.summary: Matt breaks down the AI tools he's actually kept using daily in 2026 versus the ones that were just hype. Covers Claude Code, Cursor, and several lesser-known tools for content creation and research automation.

youtube.title: Why Every Developer Should Learn Systems Design in 2026
youtube.creator: Fireship
youtube.summary: A rapid-fire overview of why systems design skills are becoming essential as AI handles more routine coding. Covers distributed systems, caching strategies, and how to think about scale.

youtube.title: I Tried Living With Only AI Assistants for a Week
youtube.creator: MKBHD
youtube.summary: Marques tests the latest AI assistants from Apple, Google, and Amazon in a week-long experiment. Mostly a rehash of known limitations with some entertaining moments but little new insight.
